title: Toiyabea alpina
intro: Toiyabea alpina, also known as Toiyabe's alpine or mountain gold, is a flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and protecting against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Toiyabea alpina include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
taxonomy: Toiyabea alpina is a rare alpine shrub endemic to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the family Santalaceae. The genus Toiyabea is monotypic, meaning it contains only one species. Toiyabea alpina is often classified as a member of the order Santalales, a group of parasitic or hemiparasitic plants. This unique classification highlights the plant's unique life cycle and adaptation to its alpine environment.
